name: The Sub-Zero Buyer
description: Factor Loy has traced the black crystal buyer: delivery to sub-zero levels of the Hive, no manifest. Hen on tier six has seen unlisted materials moving on those lifts for years. She might know what comes back up.
accept_text: He puts the box away with the specific care of someone who has decided it's important. 'Sub-zero holding facility, no manifest.' He says it. 'I've been moving cargo through the Hive for eleven years. Sub-zero level deliveries go through a specific service route — two lifts on the east side of the Under-District that don't stop at numbered tiers. I know this because I've been paid not to ask questions about them three times.' He looks at you. 'The workers on the numbered tiers adjacent to those lifts see the materials moving. They don't ask questions because the materials are on those lifts, which is the signal that questions are not welcome.' He is quiet. 'There's a tier-six conveyor worker — her name is Hen. She's been on that tier for twelve years. The east-side lifts pass her conveyor junction. She sees everything that moves.' He meets your eyes. 'I can't ask her directly. I don't have Hive-tier access and I don't have a reason to be there that wouldn't make her cautious.' He pauses. 'You can move through the Hive in ways I can't. Go to tier six. Find Hen. Tell her: someone is delivering pre-Cascade stone to sub-zero levels. Ask her if she's seen it. Ask her if she's seen what comes back up.' He pauses. 'And ask her — separately — if she's noticed any change in the sub-zero activity in the last nine months. Not eight. Nine. Before the Grid reactivated.'
complete_text: Hen is quiet when you describe the material. Not the quiet of someone who doesn't know what you're talking about. The quiet of someone deciding how much to say. 'Nine months ago,' she says. 'The east lift traffic doubled. I noticed because the junction calibration drifts when there's higher-than-standard load frequency on the structure.' She makes the minor adjustment she makes every four hours, automatic. 'The material coming up on the return runs —' She looks at her hands. 'I don't know what it is. It's in sealed cases that I've seen on the lift seven or eight times. Standard dimensions, nothing on the exterior to identify content.' She pauses. 'What comes back up from below, generally: finished components. Things that went down as raw material and came back refined.' She looks at the junction. 'Nine months ago, the cases started coming up already sealed, which is different from before. Before, the cases were open on the way down and sealed on the way up. Like they were loading something into them below.' She meets your eyes. 'I also noticed: the workers who go below started coming back different before nine months ago. The change in the workers started about eleven months ago. The change in the lift traffic started nine months ago.' She looks at the lift. 'Eleven months before the Grid reactivated, something started happening in the sub-zero levels. Nine months ago, it started producing output.' She is quiet. 'Whatever they were building down there — I think they finished.'
